templates.name: Mean Reversion (RSI + Z-Score)
templates.description: Buy oversold dips, sell overbought peaks. Combines RSI with rolling Z-Score for high-conviction reversals. Best in ranging markets — pair with preferred_regimes: ["RANGE_BOUND"] for live use.

templates.name: Trend Following (MACD)
templates.description: Long-only trend follower. Enters when MACD line is positive — a coarse but reliable trend proxy. The full named strategy in the partner brief used MA cross-overs which our indicator set doesn't ship; MACD signal direction is the closest equivalent we have today.

templates.name: Bollinger Mean Reversion ( ± 2σ)
templates.description: Long when price punches below the lower Bollinger Band ( ± 2σ); short when it pierces the upper band. Uses the bb.pct_b indicator so the rule fires on band penetration regardless of absolute price level. Works in both ranging and mildly trending regimes.

templates.name: High Volume Breakout
templates.description: Long-only breakout filter. Fires when relative volume (rvol) exceeds 2x the recent average AND RSI is in a momentum range. Approximates the partner brief's VWAP Breakout idea — full "close above VWAP" semantics need a price-vs-indicator DSL extension, not yet shipped.

_notes: Templates intentionally avoid features the existing rule DSL doesn't support: indicator-vs-indicator comparisons, price/close as a queryable indicator, and named moving averages (MA50/MA200). The brief's original Volatility Squeeze and full VWAP Breakout templates require those extensions and are not shipped yet.
preferred_regimes lives in the canonical strategy_rules JSON (added by C.4). The hot-path short-circuits a profile with shadow=true when the live regime is not in this list.

entry_long / entry_short shape was added by C.1. Profiles using both-legs share a single confidence value and a single allocation; the hot-path emits BUY when only the long leg matches, SELL when only the short, and nothing (with a warning) when both match in the same tick.
